rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DOUGLAS P. AMOS GST EXEMPT TRUST, DATED MARCH 24, 2010
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petition For An Order Under Probate Code Section 850 Directing Payment Of The California Department Of State Hospitals Creditors Claim
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn: Before the court are the Trustee's demurrers to DSH's various Petitions for an Order Under Probate Code Section 850 Directing Payment of DHS's Creditor's Claim and related Requests for Judicial Notice. The unopposed requests for judicial notice are GRANTED. The court takes notice of relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented. The demurrers are OVERRULED. The issue is whether assuming true the facts properly pled and facts properly noticed, do DSH's claim for reimbursement necessarily fail. (See Code Civ. Proc., §430.10(e); Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) The Trustee has not established as a matter of law that "the terms of the relevant trust documents do not permit funds from the Trust to be used to pay for Douglas' regular, non-emergency care in State Hospitals." (Notice of Dem. at p. 2.) DSH has alleged or the noticeable facts show that Douglas was a patient in a state hospital for the mentally disordered, Douglas became a patient in the state hospital pursuant to the Welfare and Institutions Code and the state incurred costs for Douglas' care, support and maintenance in the state hospital; thus, Douglas's estate may be held liable for those costs. (See Welf. & Inst. Code, § 7275.) "Estate" includes a person's beneficial interest in a trust established for his benefit. (In re Lackmann's Estate (1958) 156 Cal.App.2d 674.) Even if the trust is a spendthrift trust, it is not settled that a court may not find that the Trustee's discretion must be exercised in favor of reimbursing the state. (See Ibid.; see also In re Johnson's Estate (1961) 198 Cal.App.2d 503; Prob. Code, §15306.) Applicability of Welfare and Institutions 15306, subdivision (b) turns on facts not settled on this record. Nor is it settled that "the State is responsible for [the cost of] Douglas' confinement in State Hospitals following his confinement." (Notice of Dem. at p. 2.) For example, the patient's estate may be liable for the costs of care under Section 7275. (See also Dep't of Developmental Servs. v. Ladd (1990) 224 Cal.App.3d 128, 138.) Douglas was not confined in state prison, nor was he an inmate transferred to a state hospital; thus, Conservatorship of Edde (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 883 is not controlling. The Trustee's issue-preclusion argument likewise fails. The Trustee did not establish the prerequisites for issue preclusion. For example, the issues resolved in the Matter of the Amos Revocable Trust (PTR-09-292137) ("2009 Proceeding") and the issues presented in the pending petitions are not identical. The 2009 Proceeding focused on whether the Amos Trust, as a proxy for his parents, or the sub-trusts could be held liable for the costs of Douglas' care, and whether that liability could be paid out of funds that were otherwise designated to fund his special needs trust, whereas in the pending petitions DSH seeks reimbursement under Welf. & Inst. Code § 7275. DSH's predecessor raised Section 7275 in a motion to set aside the order issued in the 2009 Proceeding but the Trustee has not established-and the court's review does not show-that the court necessarily based its order denying the set-aside motion on Section 7275 or any issue related to that section. In the 2009 Proceeding, DSH's predecessor argued Section 7275 in an opposition to an accounting, but withdrew this opposition. Moreover, on demurrer, the court will not exercise any equitable authority to bar DSH's claim. DSH to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on August 20, 2019.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SOFIA HOFFMARK DECLARATION TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Validity Of Trust; Confirming Successor Trustee; And Confirming Asset To Revocable Trust
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required for the court to determine whether to grant the petition and confirm property is held in trust. Counsel may be asked to address why the agent under the DPOA created the trust when the Settlor was still living and to address the Settlor's knowledge of the actions taken by her agent. Counsel may also be asked to address the trust having different beneficiaries if Michael predeceased Sofia versus the terms in Sofia's Will, and Probate Code section 4264(f).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALFRED LORD JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, LIMITED APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1. First appearance on competing petitions for appointment. 2.
